mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflect expanding aggregate income and consumption expectations, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not positive in greater than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0%, further tightening reduces present values of future corporate cash flows through a higher discount rate, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in fewer than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0% over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals stagflationary supply constraints that constrain Federal Reserve flexibility and erode corporate earnings, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change > 0 and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: When both CPIAUCSL is rising and UNRATE is rising, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of occurrences over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, quarterly real GDP (GDPC1) growth decelerating below its 4-quarter moving average signals aggregate demand saturation, prompting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 is below its 4-quarter moving average during late-cycle expansions, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not negative in greater than 55% of triggered instances over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals a sudden pull-back in consumer spending that immediately reduces near-term revenue visibility, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month RSAFS declines are negative in fewer than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) falling below its 12-month trailing level signals capacity underutilization and weakening manufacturing profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of instances when INDPRO falls below its 12-month lag over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint weakness in quarterly real GDP (GDPC1) and monthly payrolls (PAYEMS) confirms a broad aggregate demand contraction that reduces corporate earnings across sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 0% quarter-over-quarter and PAYEMS month-over-month change < 0
failure_condition: When both GDPC1 growth is negative and PAYEMS declines month-over-month, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not negative in greater than 60% of instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) by more than 0.2 percentage points over two months signals deteriorating household income and falling consumption, triggering rapid risk-off and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following a 0.2pp UNRATE increase are negative in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.
